segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translation.text: 听录音，然后回答下面的问题。

segments.text: How many unsuccessful attempts did Mendoza make before becoming champion of all England?

translation.text: 门多萨在成为全英拳击冠军之前，经历了几次失败？

segments.text: matches were very popular in England 200 years ago.

translation.text: 200年前，拳击比赛在英国非常流行。

segments.text: In those days, boxers fought with bare fists for prize money.

translation.text: 当时，拳击手赤手空拳比赛，以赢取奖金。

segments.text: Because of this, they were known as prize fighters.

translation.text: 因此，他们被称为职业拳击手。

segments.text: However, boxing was very crude, for there were no rules and a prize fighter could be seriously injured or even killed during a match.

translation.text: 然而，那时的拳击十分野蛮，因为没有规则，职业拳击手可能在比赛中身受重伤甚至丧命。

segments.text: One of the most colorful figures in boxing history was Daniel Mendoza, who was born in 1764.

translation.text: 拳击史上最富传奇色彩的人物之一，是1764年出生的丹尼尔·门多萨。

segments.text: The use of gloves was not introduced until 1860, when the Marquis of Queensbury drew up the first set of rules.

translation.text: 直到1860年昆斯伯里侯爵制定出第一套规则后，拳击手套才开始使用。

segments.text: Though he was technically a prize fighter, Mendoza did much to change crude prize fighting into a sport, for he brought science to the game.

translation.text: 尽管严格说来也是职业拳击手，门多萨却为把野蛮的奖金拳击改造成一项运动做出了很大贡献，因为他把科学技巧带进了比赛。

segments.text: In his day, Mendoza enjoyed tremendous popularity.

translation.text: 门多萨在当时极受欢迎。

segments.text: He was adored by rich and poor alike.

translation.text: 无论穷人还是富人都崇拜他。

segments.text: Mendoza rose to fame swiftly after a boxing match when he was only 14 years old.

translation.text: 门多萨14岁参加一场拳击赛后迅速成名。

segments.text: This attracted the attention of Richard Humphreys, who was then the most eminent boxer in England.

translation.text: 这引起了理查德·汉弗莱斯的注意，他当时是英国最杰出的拳击手。

segments.text: He offered to train Mendoza, and his young pupil was quick to learn.

translation.text: 他提出训练门多萨，这位年轻学生学得很快。

segments.text: In fact, Mendoza soon became so successful that Humphreys turned against him.

translation.text: 事实上，门多萨很快便取得巨大成功，以至于汉弗莱斯反目成仇。

segments.text: The two men quarreled bitterly, and it was clear that the argument could only be settled by a fight.

translation.text: 两人激烈争吵，显然只有一场拳赛才能解决争端。

segments.text: A match was held at Stilton, where both men fought for an hour.

translation.text: 比赛在斯蒂尔顿举行，两人打了一个小时。

segments.text: The public bet a great deal of money on Mendoza, but he was defeated.

translation.text: 公众在门多萨身上下了重注，但他输了。

segments.text: Mendoza met Humphreys in the ring on a later occasion and he lost for a second time.

translation.text: 后来门多萨再次在拳台上迎战汉弗莱斯，第二次失利。

segments.text: It was not until his third match, in 1790, that he finally beat Humphreys and became champion of England.

translation.text: 直到1790年的第三场比赛，他才终于击败汉弗莱斯，成为全英冠军。

segments.text: Meanwhile, he founded a highly successful academy, and even Lord Byron became one of his pupils.

translation.text: 与此同时，他创办了一所非常成功的拳击学校，甚至连拜伦勋爵也成了他的学生。

segments.text: He earned enormous sums of money and was paid as much as £100 for a single appearance.

translation.text: 他赚取巨额收入，单次出场费最高可达100英镑。

segments.text: Despite this, he was so extravagant that he was always in debt.

translation.text: 尽管如此，他挥霍无度，总是负债累累。

segments.text: After he was defeated by a boxer called Gentleman Jackson, he was quickly forgotten.

translation.text: 被一位名叫“绅士杰克逊”的拳击手击败后，他很快被人遗忘。

segments.text: He was sent to prison for failing to pay his debts, and died in poverty in 1836.

translation.text: 他因无力偿债被送进监狱，并于1836年贫困潦倒地死去。
